title: Port Changes Mutate
description: TypeScript surface for **Port Changes Mutate** on `SpecdDataPort`, mirroring the HTTP routes in the paired `api:routes-*` group. UI hooks call these methods; adapters implement them over HTTP or IPC.
context: TypeScript surface for **Port Changes Mutate** on `SpecdDataPort`, mirroring the HTTP routes in the paired `api:routes-*` group. UI hooks call these methods; adapters implement them over HTTP or IPC.

rules:
The interface MUST declare asynchronous methods equivalent to the HTTP routes in the matching `api:routes-*` group:

- `saveChangeArtifact(name, filename, { content, originalHash, force? })`
- `validateChange(...)` — single step (`POST .../validate`)
- `validateChangeAll(name, { artifactId? })` — DAG batch (`POST .../validate-all`)
- `transitionChange(...)`, lifecycle posts, `patchChange(...)`
- `updateSpecDependencies(name, { specId, set | add | remove })` → `PATCH .../spec-dependencies`
- `updateImplementationTracking(name, { action, file, specId?, symbols? })` → `PATCH .../implementation-tracking`

`updateSpecDependencies(...)` MUST return `{ specId, dependsOn }`.
`updateImplementationTracking(...)` MUST return `{ implementationTracking }`.

Drafted and discarded are read-only storage classes; lifecycle mutations are still addressed by change name:

- `draftChange(name)` → `POST /changes/{name}/draft`
- `restoreChange(name)` → `POST /changes/{name}/restore`
- `discardChange(name)` → `POST /changes/{name}/discard`
- `archiveChange(name)` → `POST /changes/{name}/archive`

rules.requirement: port signatures are identical for HTTP and IPC adapters
rules: Implementations (`adapter-remote-specd-data`, desktop IPC) MUST implement these methods without altering parameter or return types.

rules.requirement: port failures surface as typed client errors
rules: HTTP failures MUST be translated by `adapter-problem-json-errors` into errors the UI hooks can display.

constraints: HTTP handlers MUST NOT import @specd/core from @specd/ui or @specd/client.
v1 server auth: api.auth.type from specd.yaml (never studio.*); registry registers only disabled; no server-side Bearer enforcement on loopback or specd ui serve.
Artifact save/load MUST use core:save-change-artifact and core:get-change-artifact — not raw ChangeRepository.saveArtifact from HTTP handlers.
There is no GET /changes/{name}/validation resource; use GET .../status and POST .../validate.
Canonical workspace spec artifacts are read-only in Studio v1.

scenarios.name: Port methods mirror routes contract
then: each method maps to documented /v1 route

scenarios.name: Typed patch helpers preserve result DTOs
then: adapters return the documented mutation result DTOs
AND callers do not need Record<string, unknown> casts

scenarios.name: List changes hits collection HTTP route
when: listChanges() is called on remote adapter
then: AND response parses to typed rows

scenarios.name: IPC adapter exposes same method names
when: desktop IPC adapter handles port call
then: method name and arity match interface
AND return shape matches HTTP path

scenarios.requirement: port signatures are identical for HTTP and IPC adapters
scenarios.name: Remote and IPC adapters share types
when: TypeScript compiles both adapters against port interface

scenarios.requirement: port signatures are identical for HTTP and IPC adapters
scenarios.name: UI hooks do not branch on transport
then: same method signature for HTTP and IPC

scenarios.name: Signature drift fails build

scenarios.name: HTTP 404 becomes typed client error

scenarios.name: Network failure is not swallowed
